working_definition: A basal keratinocyte of the fetal interfollicular epidermis distinguished by expression of periostin (POSTN), present throughout gestation (7-17 PCW). Positioned at the origin of dual differentiation trajectories: the ORS/CL trajectory via DPYSL2+ basal cells, and the IRS trajectory via placode and matrix cells. Increased cellular migration in POSTN+ basal cells may be involved in placode specification and dermal invagination during hair follicle morphogenesis.

best_match.cl_definition: A mitotically active, columnar-shaped keratinocyte located in the stratum basale of the epidermis and attached to the basement membrane.

justification: CL:0002187 is the closest existing term but does not capture the POSTN expression, migratory phenotype, or the dual-trajectory progenitor capacity unique to this fetal population. This is a broad match; the POSTN+ basal cell is a specialized subtype of basal epidermal cell.
recommendation: Use CL:0002187 (basal cell of epidermis) as a broad match. A new subtype term for POSTN-expressing fetal basal keratinocyte progenitor could be considered.
